behavior_summary: U8P_GRAVEII::look is the object's examine/U8P_GRAVEII::look handler: when the player inspects the gravestone it emits a short player-facing bark "gravestone " and then the U8P_GRAVEII::look process ends (suspend).

U8P_GRAVEII::use is the object's U8P_GRAVEII::use/interaction handler: it delegates the concrete interaction to an external routine GRAVE_NS::func00CB and then suspends. The external routine implements the actual effects of using the gravestone.
No global flags or local variables are read or written in this excerpt; behavior is minimal and mostly delegated to the external call for the U8P_GRAVEII::use action.
notable_control_flow: U8P_GRAVEII::look: single linear flow — emit bark "gravestone " then suspend/terminate the process.
U8P_GRAVEII::use: single linear flow — call GRAVE_NS::func00CB then suspend/terminate. No branching or menu logic present in either function.
open_questions: What exactly GRAVE_NS::func00CB does when called by U8P_GRAVEII::use (display text, change state, open menu, spawn entity, set flags, etc.)?
Under which precise player actions or engine events is U8P_GRAVEII::use invoked in gameplay (player 'U8P_GRAVEII::use' command, context menu, scripted event, etc.)?
Are there other scripts or game systems that set or read flags related to this gravestone that are not visible in this excerpt?

main_function.name: U8P_GRAVEII::look
main_function.purpose: Provide the short description when the player examines/looks at the gravestone and then end the U8P_GRAVEII::look process.
entry_conditions: Triggered by the player's 'U8P_GRAVEII::look' or 'examine' action on the gravestone object.
May also be invoked as part of the engine's object-inspection/enter-area flow (inferred).
